title: Study of RMC-9805 in Participants With KRAS G12D-Mutant Solid Tumors

eligibility.text: Pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic solid tumor with a KRAS G12D-mutation
eligibility.justification: This criterion is designed to ensure the study population has a specific disease characteristic that will allow for comparison of treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Received and progressed or been intolerant to prior standard therapy (including targeted therapy) appropriate for tumor type and stage
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient has a history of treatment failure, which may impact their ability to tolerate further interventions.
This criterion is designed to ensure the patient can participate in the study and adhere to its requirements.
This criterion ensures the patient has a baseline level of health that allows them to tolerate treatment and follow-up procedures.
This criterion is designed to ensure the study population has a specific disease characteristic that will allow for comparison of treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Known or suspected leptomeningeal or active brain metastases or spinal cord compression
eligibility.justification: This criterion is designed to protect the patient from known harm by excluding those with a high risk of complications.

eligibility.text: Known or suspected impairment of gastrointestinal function that may prohibit ability to swallow or absorb an oral medication
eligibility.justification: This criterion is designed to ensure the patient can participate in the study and adhere to its requirements.

eligibility.text: Participant was previously treated with an investigational KRAS G12D inhibitor, pan- or multi-RAS inhibitor, or had prior therapy with any direct RAS-targeted therapy (eg, degraders and inhibitors)
eligibility.justification: This criterion is designed to protect the patient from known harm by excluding those who have already experienced adverse effects from similar treatments.
This criterion is a general exclusionary clause that allows for the inclusion of additional factors that may impact patient safety or study feasibility.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

endpoints.text: Maximum Observed Blood Concentration (Cmax) of RMC-9805 as monotherapy and in combination with RMC-6236, and Cmax of RMC-6236 in combination with RMC-9805
Time to Reach Maximum Blood Concentration (Tmax) of RMC-9805 as monotherapy and in combination with RMC-6236, and Tmax of RMC-6236 in combination with RMC-9805
Area Under Blood Concentration Time Curve (AUC) of RMC-9805 as monotherapy and in combination with RMC-6236, and AUC of RMC-6236 in combination with RMC-9805

Ratio of accumulation of RMC-9805 from a single dose to steady state with repeated dosing as monotherapy and in combination with RMC-6236, and ratio of accumulation of RMC-6236 in combination with RMC-9805
Elimination Half-Life (t1/2) of RMC-9805 as monotherapy and in combination with RMC-6236, and t1/2 of RMC-6236 in combination with RMC-9805

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

known_effects: Treatment response - KRAS G12D mutation required

pharmacogenetics.genetic_screen_failure_summary: Detected 1 genetic biomarker requirement(s) in eligibility: KRAS_G12D (pop. prevalence ~5%, ~95% screen fail). Worst-case screen failure rate from genetic criteria: ~95%.
pharmacogenetics.summary: Genes detected (KRAS, NRAS, HRAS) but could not map indication "non-small cell lung cancer" to a GWAS EFO trait. Try alternative trait name.
